headline: Consumer Tech News (Dec 15-19): Meta Accelerates AI Wearables Push, Trump Unveils U.S. Air Taxi Strategy, US Halts Technology Agreement With UK & More
summary: White House AI czar David Sacks says AI is driving U.S. hiring and wage growth, challenging claims it threatens American jobs. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy reveals Trump administration plan to launch U.S. air taxi operations and expand urban mobility.

headline: Shaq Declined an $80,000 Expense, Then Turned It Into a Million-Dollar Win
summary: Shaquille O'Neal has demonstrated that his business acumen is as impressive as his basketball skills. O'Neal's decision to opt for a cost-effective security solution for his property has resulted in a lucrative investment.

headline: How a College Roommate's $1,000 Loan Inspired MacKenzie Scott's Approach To Giving Away Billions
summary: The philanthropic spirit of MacKenzie Scott, was sparked by a gesture of kindness from her college roommate.

headline: Musk's Net Worth Nears Three-Quarters of a Trillion Following Landmark Pay Decision
summary: Elon Musk has seen his net worth surge to a staggering $749 billion.

headline: Rivian, Sandisk, And Moderna Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (Dec. 15-Dec. 19): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
summary: Top large-cap gainers for Dec. 15–19 were led by Rivian, Moderna, and BioMarin, rising on product/software updates, vaccine funding news, and a major acquisition deal, with Rocket Lab, Affirm, and cruise names also jumping on strong contracts, partnerships, and earnings.

headline: Bill Gates Was Introduced To LSD By This Person At Microsoft — It Is None Other Than The Guy Who Also Got Him Drunk And Gave Him Pot
summary: Bill Gates revealed that Microsoft co-founder Paul Allen, who profoundly influenced his early life, introduced him to LSD, marijuana and alcohol during his teens, experiences Gates now views with caution despite their role in his curiosity-driven youth.

headline: Nike, Insmed, And Arm Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (Dec. 15-Dec. 19): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
summary: Last week's biggest large-cap laggards were Nike, Arm, and Lennar, sliding on weak China demand, analyst downgrades, earnings/CFO headlines, and broader crypto/energy pressure.

headline: Japan's App Law, Eight New iPhones, Foldable iPhone Design And More: This Week In Appleverse
summary: Apple and Google face visa delays, regulatory changes, new iPhone plans, and potential sales slowdown, highlighting a turbulent weekend for tech giants.

headline: Trump's $550 Billion Japan Trade Deal In Doubt, Inflation Data Credibility Questioned And More: This Week In Economy
summary: Experts skeptical of full implementation of Trump's $550B Japan trade deal, November inflation data questioned, Musk acknowledges China's massive market, and US jobs data disappoints with slowing growth and rising unemployment.

headline: Elon Musk Calls Tesla Optimus Rival's Dancing Robots 'Impressive' As Unitree Machines Perform Flips, Acrobatic Stunts
summary: Elon Musk admires Unitree robots' performance, including Webster flips and dance routines, at a concert.

headline: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk Says Multiplanetary Life To Ensure Long-Term Survival Of Consciousness Will Be An Expensive Affair
summary: Elon Musk emphasizes costly financial challenges of making life multiplanetary for the survival of consciousness.

headline: Mike Repole Says 'I Spend More Time Talking People Out Of Being An Entrepreneur,' Warns Of Daily Bankruptcy Risks In First 5 'Survival Years'
summary: Mike Repole, beverage mogul who sold Glaceau and BodyArmor to Coca-Cola, cautions that entrepreneurship is riskier than portrayed.

headline: PG&E Substation Fire Triggers Massive San Francisco Outage, Impacting Over 130,000 Customers
summary: A major PG&E power outage left over 130,000 San Francisco customers without electricity, disrupting transit, traffic signals.

headline: Bill Ackman Proposes Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO Through SPARC Structure, Offering Tesla Shareholders Priority Access
summary: Bill Ackman proposes merging Elon Musk's SpaceX with his SPARC vehicle, offering Tesla shareholders priority access.

headline: Google, Apple Warn Visa Employees Against Leaving US As 'Significant" Delays Push Backlogs To Nearly 12 Months: Report
summary: Alphabet, Google, and Apple advise visa-holding employees to avoid travel due to long wait times for consular appointments.
